creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
PRs are cheap and public, and appropriate if you have patches.  If it's just "hey, there's a newer version available" then PRs are not appropriate.

Especially if it's on sourceforge, the maintainers already know about updated versions availability because portscout sends an email and tracks it.

Anyway, it's fine.  office@ knows now and can close the PR if they want.
